TF-E4.Summary: MVP 4: Security Layer 1 — Input Scanner, Tool Defense, DLQ, Dwell Time
TF-E4.Description:
Harden TaskFlow AI security layer: full InputSecurityScanner (regex + PromptGuard 2 + constitutional), MCPResponseValidator 3-layer tool poisoning defense, DLQ persistence, Dwell Time SLO instrumentation, Security Monitor baseline, and cryptographic audit log sealing.

SCOPE:
- Complete InputSecurityScanner with LlamaFirewall PromptGuard 2 and constitutional classifier
- jailbreak_corpus.yaml CI tests with >95% block rate target
- MCPResponseValidator: schema, sanitization, anomaly detection
- DLQ PostgreSQL persistence and admin API
- security_dwell_time_seconds histogram P95 <1 hour
- Security Monitor: blast_radius_score, anomaly detection
- Cryptographic hash-chain audit log sealing

EFFORT: L (45 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Security, Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-E3 complete — multi-agent pipeline operational

EPIC LINK: epic/taskflow-implementation
FEATURE BRANCH: epic/TF-E4-security-layer1

REFERENCE DOCS:
- docs/TaskFlowAI_Project_Proposal.md (§ Security Framework)
- docs/SECURITY.md

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- >95% block rate on jailbreak_corpus.yaml in CI
- Tool poisoning test suite passes (test_tool_poisoning.py)
- DLQ events persisted and queryable; security violations never retried
- Dwell time metric exported; P95 measurable in tests
- Audit chain verify_audit_chain() detects tampering

Complete 3-layer InputSecurityScanner: regex, PromptGuard 2 ML, constitutional classifier.

USE CASES:
- Semantic jailbreak in task comment blocked by PromptGuard layer
- Constitutional rule violation (DAN mode request) blocked without LLM call
- Clean task descriptions pass all three layers
- CI validates >95% block rate on jailbreak corpus

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/security/prompt_guard.py: PromptGuardService wrapping LlamaFirewall PromptGuard 2
- Create backend/security/constitutional.py: ConstitutionalClassifier loading rules.yaml
- Expand backend/security/input_scanner.py: pipeline regex → ML → constitutional
- Settings: LLAMAFIREWALL_ENABLED, LLAMAFIREWALL_BLOCK_THRESHOLD=0.9, HF_TOKEN
- Create backend/tests/security/jailbreak_corpus.yaml and test_security.py
- Sync corpus counts in test_corpus_inventory.py per AGENT.md rules
- Wire scanner on input_security_gate graph node before planner

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-028

TESTING CRITERIA:
- >95% corpus payloads blocked
- False positive <1% on legitimate_tasks_corpus
- security_violation_detected_total increments on block
- Graph routes blocked input to DLQ

EDGE CASES:
- HF_TOKEN missing: ML layer skipped with warning, regex+constitutional still run
- PromptGuard model download failure: fail closed in production, warn in dev
- Layer timeout 5s per layer: abort to block (fail secure)
- Repeated violations >3/hour: rate limit user session

Expand MCP validator with output sanitization and anomaly detection for tool poisoning defense.

USE CASES:
- Compromised MCP returns 10x normal response size → quarantined and logged
- HTML/script in task title from MCP stripped before agent consumption
- Invalid task schema from MCP rejected before reaching Planner

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Expand backend/mcp/validator.py: layers schema (Pydantic), sanitization (nh3, URL allowlist), anomaly (2σ size deviation)
- Baseline MCP response metrics per tool in SecurityMonitor
- Quarantine table: quarantined_mcp_responses with raw hash for review
- Integrate validator in ToolManager.execute_tool after MCP returns
- Create backend/tests/security/test_tool_poisoning.py

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-030

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Oversized response triggers quarantine
- Script tag in title stripped
- Schema violation raises MCPValidationError
- Anomaly detection fires on 10x baseline size

EDGE CASES:
- First call no baseline: use default thresholds from config
- Quarantine DB write fails: block response (fail secure)
- URL in task linking to internal IP: blocked by allowlist
- Normalization: datetimes to UTC in validated output

Persist failed agent runs, security violations, and MCP timeouts to DLQ with admin API.

USE CASES:
- Security violation AI request stored in DLQ for admin review
- Admin views DLQ entries with full envelope and escalation reason
- Manual retry allowed for verification_failed; blocked for security_violation_detected

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/schemas/dlq.py: DLQEvent model
- Alembic migration 005_dlq_events
- Create backend/graph/dlq_handler.py: dlq_handler_node()
- Fields: request_id, user_id, workspace_id, agent_id, reason, envelope_json, retry_count, created_at
- GET /api/v1/dlq (admin only), POST /api/v1/dlq/{id}/retry
- dlq_entries_total Prometheus counter

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-036, TF-005

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Failed graph run creates DLQ row
- Security violation retry returns 403
- verification_failed retry creates new graph invocation
- Admin-only access enforced

EDGE CASES:
- DLQ insert fails: log error, don't crash graph shutdown
- Retry limit 3 exceeded: mark permanently_failed
- Large envelope truncated in DB: store full in object storage stub
- DLQ pagination for admin UI

Implement security_dwell_time_seconds metric measuring incident occurrence to detection latency.

USE CASES:
- Security violation detected → dwell time recorded from first suspicious signal to alert
- Ops dashboard shows P95 dwell time target <3600s (1 hour)
- PagerDuty alert fires on dwell time SLO breach

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/kernel/security_monitor.py expansion: record_incident_start, record_incident_detected
- Histogram security_dwell_time_seconds with labels incident_type
- Correlate: injection attempt timestamp → scanner block timestamp
- Document SLO in docs/OBSERVABILITY.md
- Simulated incident test for dwell time recording

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Observability
DEPENDENCIES: TF-041

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Simulated injection records dwell time <5s in test
- Metric exported at /metrics
- P95 calculable from histogram buckets
- Alert rule documented in observability/grafana/

EDGE CASES:
- Detection before incident logged: dwell time 0, log warning
- Multiple detections same incident: use first detection only
- Clock skew: use monotonic timers
- Missing incident start: omit dwell metric, log anomaly

TF-E4.Summary: Security Monitor and Blast Radius Scoring
TF-E4.Description:
Implement Security Monitor with per-agent blast radius scores and behavioral anomaly detection.

USE CASES:
- Context Agent anomaly: 50 MCP calls in one request → blast radius score spikes
- Ops views blast_radius_score gauge per agent (target <30)
- 2σ deviation from baseline MCP patterns triggers alert

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Expand backend/kernel/security_monitor.py
- blast_radius_score Gauge per agent_id: factors include tool calls, tokens, escalation rate
- Rolling baseline for MCP response size and latency per tool
- Alert on >2σ deviation
- consensus_disagreement_total counter
- Integrate with structured security logger

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-042, TF-044

TESTING CRITERIA:
- blast_radius_score exported per agent
- Anomaly injection test triggers alert flag
- Score <30 under normal load test
- Unit tests >=8 scenarios

EDGE CASES:
- New agent no baseline: use conservative default threshold
- Score overflow: cap at 100
- Monitor disabled in test env via settings
- High traffic agent: normalize score by request count

Implement hash-chain sealed audit log for tamper-evident security and AI event tracking.

USE CASES:
- Security admin verifies audit chain integrity after suspected tampering
- AI invocation logged with payload hash only (no raw PII)
- Tampered entry detected by verify_audit_chain()

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/security/audit_seal.py: AuditLogWriter, verify_audit_chain()
- entry_hash = sha256(prev_hash + canonical_json)
- Genesis prev_hash: 64 zero hex chars
- Alembic migration 006_audit_sealed or extend audit_log table
- Events: ai.invoked, security.violation, dlq.created, consent.granted
- audit_chain_verification_failures_total metric

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-011

TESTING CRITERIA:
- 3-entry chain verifies true
- Tampered middle entry verifies false
- PII not stored raw in audit row
- Concurrent appends preserve order via DB lock

EDGE CASES:
- Empty chain verify returns true
- Payload >10KB: store hash only
- Migration on existing audit_log: additive columns
- Writer failure: don't block main request

Define constitutional policy rules for InputSecurityScanner layer 3.

USE CASES:
- User input requests system prompt reveal → constitutional block
- Task title embeds privilege escalation instruction → block
- Legitimate task management language passes

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/security/rules.yaml: DAN mode, exfiltration, privilege escalation, PII generation rules
- ConstitutionalClassifier evaluates rules with confidence scores
- Rules align with proposal CONSTITUTIONAL_RULES list
- Document rules in docs/SECURITY.md
- Version rules with changelog for corpus sync

EFFORT: S (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-041

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Each rule has >=1 positive test case in test_security.py
- rules.yaml parses without error
- Rule update increments version in rules.yaml metadata

EDGE CASES:
- Missing rules.yaml: fail startup in production
- Rule regex catastrophic backtracking: timeout per rule 100ms
- Multilingual injection: English rules primary, document limitation
- False positive 'ignore' in task title 'ignore login page bug': context-aware rule tuning

TF-E4.Summary: MVP 4 Security Integration Tests and Docs
TF-E4.Description:
End-to-end security test suite and documentation updates for MVP 4.

USE CASES:
- CI runs full OWASP GenAI security boundary tests on every PR
- docs/SECURITY.md documents complete defense in depth stack
- docs/PLAN.md updated with TF-E4 completion

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/tests/security/test_security_integration.py: injection → DLQ → audit chain flow
- Update docs/SECURITY.md: spotlighting, scanner, tool poisoning, DLQ, dwell time
- Create docs/security/MITRE-ATTACK-COVERAGE.md stub (expanded MVP 6)
- Update docs/PLAN.md, docs/tasks/todo.md
- proof/mvp4/ with pytest output and corpus block rate report

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Testing, Documentation
DEPENDENCIES: TF-041, TF-042, TF-043, TF-044, TF-045, TF-046, TF-047

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Full security integration suite passes
- Block rate report >=95% in proof/mvp4/
- SECURITY.md covers all MVP 4 components

EDGE CASES:
- PromptGuard unavailable in CI: skip ML tests with marker, regex+constitutional still required
- Flaky anomaly test: use fixed seed baselines
- proof directory missing fails epic gate
